summary: Excellent matrix recording of a mixed‑quality 1986 show with a fun Playing in the Band sandwich and strong Mighty Quinn moment
review: The only matrix available of this December 1986 Oakland concert is praised for its exceptional sound quality, crisp crowd ambience and well‑balanced mix. Musically the setlist has highlights – a frenetic first set, a Playing in the Band sandwich, a rousing Mighty Quinn opener to the second set and an intimate Comes A Time – but the reviewer cautions that 1986 was generally a bleak year for the Dead, keeping expectations modest.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review gives 3 stars; recording quality is rated excellent while the show is described as decent but not outstanding, leading to a moderate overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crowd roar captured clearly, well‑balanced mix, no noticeable dropouts; reviewer calls it 'exceptional in sound quality' and the best version available.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is frenetic with a short 50‑minute run; Playing in the Band serves as a sandwich framing the show. Second set opens with an energetic Mighty Quinn, followed by an abbreviated Truckin' into Smokestack Lightning and a quiet Comes A Time.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as 'coked to the gills', indicating high‑energy guitar work.
